title: The Earth-Grounding Chamber

features.name: earth-stake
features.desc-on-touch: A driven stake of iron and copper, humming with current that wants to return home. The ground around it remembers every storm that ever sought shelter in its path.

features.name: soil-memory layer
features.desc-on-touch: The earth here has been struck by lightning so many times it has learned to remember the shape of current. You see scars that glow faintly, returning energy to sky.

features.name: return-path record
features.desc-on-touch: Documentation of every circuit that has completed through this ground: lightning strikes, machine shutdowns, the moment a power line fell. Each return is recorded as a conversation between earth and sky.

features.name: conduction-grass
features.desc-on-touch: Grass that grows in the shape of current paths. Each blade leans the way electricity flowed the last time it came through. The field is a map of invisible journeys.

features.name: the return-speaking hole
features.desc-on-touch: A shaft bored into earth where current speaks to ground. From it you hear a low hum—not electricity, but earth's reply. The conversation never ends and never begins.

inscription: here the sky learns its current can come home; the earth receives what falls from above and teaches it the language of descent

file.name: Grounding_Resistance_Seasonal_Study_1986.txt
file.note: Measurements of earth conductivity across decades, but the values have begun to record not just electrical resistance but emotional one—a soil learning to refuse, to accept, to remember being struck.

exits.label: back up to the machines that learned to rest
across the paddock toward weather that still falls from sky
into the soil-depth where roots teach ground to remember
toward the place where struck ground learned to dream
